growth_records.strain: Negev Desert biological soil crust, Israel (uncultured in situ community)
growth_records.medium: Native desert biocrust soil hydrated with simulated rain (30% v/v 2H2O heavy water)
growth_records.cite: Imminger et al. Survival and rapid resuscitation permit limited productivity in desert microbial communities. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-46920-6. [PMC11519504]
growth_records.snippet: Replication times of filamentous cyanobacteria ranged from 0.96 to 21 days. ... 4.60 at% (median) within filamentous cyanobacteria after 12 h of incubation.
growth_records.notes: Photoautotrophic filamentous cyanobacteria (Microcoleus-dominated). Reported replication (doubling) time range 0.96-21 days; no single median stated. Converted endpoints: 0.96 d x 24 = 23.04 h -> mu = ln2/23.04 = 0.03009 /h; 21 d x 24 = 504 h -> mu = ln2/504 = 0.001376 /h. Growth from single-cell 2H2O incorporation via NanoSIMS. Native soil, no defined medium composition.
